title: Fuel-corrected pace residual degradation
summary: Recent F1 tyre-degradation work models lap time as fuel mass plus latent tyre pace with pit-stop resets.
rationale: Recent F1 tyre-degradation work models lap time as fuel mass plus latent tyre pace with pit-stop resets. Existing raw LapTime_Delta and median features can confuse lighter-fuel race evolution with tyre degradation.
implementation_hint: Within each Race_Year and LapNumber, compute causal field pace baselines such as median valid LapTime excluding pit laps. For each driver-stint, add LapTimeMinusFieldLapMedian, rolling residual mean/slope over last 3-5 laps, and compound-specific residual-age interactions.
expected_effect: Better ranking of laps where tyres are truly falling off versus laps that are simply slow due to global race conditions.
risk: Same-lap field statistics assume all current-lap rows are available; using future laps or pit-affected laps can leak or add noise.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
